data.topic: pty create start timeout onData onExit CommandHandle wait TypeScript
data.content:
### TypeScript: Request Timeout Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.0/commands/page.mdx

Defines a configurable timeout for API requests in milliseconds. This option can be applied to various operations to prevent indefinite waiting. The default timeout is 60 seconds.

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

// Default:
60_000 // 60 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### Request Timeout Option - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.1/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional request timeout in milliseconds for API requests. This allows for specifying how long to wait for a response before giving up. The default timeout is 30 seconds.

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

/**
 * Timeout for requests to the API in **milliseconds**.
 * @default 30_000 // 30 seconds
 */
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Run Command in Background

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background, returning a CommandHandle for later interaction. Use CommandHandle.wait to retrieve the final result. Takes the command string and optional start options.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Run Command and Wait for Result (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the sandbox and waits for it to complete execution, returning the `CommandResult`. This is suitable for short-lived commands where immediate results are needed.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Request Timeout

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/commands/page.mdx

Defines a timeout for API requests in milliseconds. This option can be applied to various operations, including command requests and starting commands. The default timeout is 30,000 milliseconds (30 seconds).

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

// Default
// 30_000 // 30 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command Timeout - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional timeout in milliseconds for a command to complete. The default timeout is 60,000 milliseconds (60 seconds).

```typescript
optional timeoutMs: number;
```

--------------------------------

### Create PTY Command Handle (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.3/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) with specified size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. It returns a CommandHandle for interacting with the PTY.

```python
from e2b import PtySize, Username, CommandHandle

def create(size: PtySize, user: Username = "user", cwd: Optional[str] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, timeout: Optional[float] = 60, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Run Command (Flexible Mode)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command with flexible options. If opts.background is true, returns a CommandHandle for background execution. Otherwise, waits for completion and returns a CommandResult.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult | CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Create PTY in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) with specified size, data handler, user, working directory, and environment variables. It returns a handle to interact with the PTY. Supports request timeouts.

```python
async def create(
        size: PtySize,
        on_data: OutputHandler[PtyOutput],
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle
```
Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal).

**Arguments**:

- `size`: Size of the PTY
- `on_data`: Callback to handle PTY data
- `user`: User to use for the PTY
- `cwd`: Working directory for the PTY
- `envs`: Environment variables for the PTY
- `timeout`: Timeout for the PTY in **seconds**
- `request_timeout`: Timeout for the request in **seconds**

**Returns**:

Handle to interact with the PTY
```

--------------------------------

### Create New PTY (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Creates a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session within the sandbox environment. It accepts creation options and returns a `CommandHandle` for interacting with the newly created PTY.

```typescript
create(opts: PtyCreateOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Request Timeout Option - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.0/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional request timeout in milliseconds for API requests. This setting allows specifying how long to wait for a response from the API before timing out. The default value is 30,000 milliseconds (30 seconds).

```typescript
requestTimeoutMs?: number;
// Default: 30_000 // 30 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### CommandStartOpts Interface - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

The `CommandStartOpts` interface defines the configuration options for starting a new command. It includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, callbacks for stdout/stderr, request timeouts, command timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Some properties have default values.

```typescript
interface CommandStartOpts {
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.4/commands/page.mdx

Initiates a new command execution within the sandbox. This function allows configuration of various aspects such as running in the background, specifying the working directory, setting environment variables, and defining callbacks for standard output and error streams. It also supports request and command timeouts, and specifies the user to run the command as. Returns a CommandHandle for managing the started command.

```typescript
startCommand(command: string,
   args?: string[] | undefined,
   opts?: CommandStartOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Command Start Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.1/commands/page.mdx

Defines options for starting a new command within the E2B environment. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, stdout/stderr callbacks, request timeout, command timeout, and the user to run the command as.

```ts
optional background: boolean;

optional cwd: string;

optional envs: Record<string, string>;

optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;

optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;

optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

optional timeoutMs: number;

optional user: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command in Background - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/commands/page.mdx

Specifies whether a command should start in the background. If true, the method returns immediately, and the command's completion can be awaited using CommandHandle.wait.

```typescript
optional background: boolean;
```

--------------------------------

### PTY Module: Create PTY (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session with specified size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. Returns a CommandHandle for interaction.

```python
def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Request Timeout Option in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.6.0/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional request timeout for API requests in milliseconds. This interface is part of broader command request options.

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

Default:
30_000 // 30 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### Command Start Options in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for initiating a new command. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, stdout/stderr callbacks, request timeouts, command timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Defaults are provided for several options.

```typescript
optional background: boolean;
```

```typescript
optional cwd: string;
// home directory of the user used to start the command
```

```typescript
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
// overrides the default environment variables from Sandbox constructor.
// Default: {}
```

```typescript
optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
```

```typescript
optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
```

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
// Default: 30_000 // 30 seconds
```

```typescript
optional timeoutMs: number;
// Default: 60_000 // 60 seconds
```

```typescript
optional user: Username;
// Default: user
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.5/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for starting a new command within the sandbox. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, standard error/output callbacks, request timeouts, command-specific timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Defaults are provided for most options.

```typescript
optional background: boolean;
optional cwd: string;
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
optional timeoutMs: number;
optional user: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Python: Create New PTY

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new Pseudo-Terminal (PTY) with specified configurations including size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. It returns a `CommandHandle` object for interacting with the new PTY. Dependencies include `typing` and specific types like `PtySize` and `Username`.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict

# Assuming PtySize and Username are defined elsewhere
# from some_module import PtySize, Username, CommandHandle

def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for starting a new command within the sandbox. This includes options for running in the background, setting the working directory, defining environment variables, and providing callbacks for standard error and standard output. It also includes request and command-specific timeouts.

```typescript
/**
 * Options for starting a new command.
 */
interface CommandStartOpts {
  /**
   * If true, starts command in the background and the method returns immediately.
   * You can use CommandHandle.wait to wait for the command to finish.
   */
  background?: boolean;

  /**
   * Working directory for the command.
   * @default // home directory of the user used to start the command
   */
  cwd?: string;

  /**
   * Environment variables used for the command.
   * This overrides the default environment variables from `Sandbox` constructor.
   * @default {}
   */
  envs?: Record<string, string>;

  /**
   * Callback for command stderr output.
   */
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;

  /**
   * Callback for command stdout output.
   */
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;

  /**
   * Timeout for requests to the API in **milliseconds**.
   * @default 30_000 // 30 seconds
   */
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;

  /**
   * Timeout for the command in **milliseconds**.
   * @default 60_000 // 60 seconds
   */
  timeoutMs?: number;

  /**
   * User to run the command as.
   * @default user
   */
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Command Timeout Option - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.0/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional timeout in milliseconds for a command's execution. This allows setting a maximum duration for a command to run before it is terminated. The default value is 60,000 milliseconds (60 seconds).

```typescript
timeoutMs?: number;
// Default: 60_000 // 60 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New PTY

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.5/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initiates a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session within the sandbox. This function allows customization of the PTY's size, user, working directory, and environment variables. It returns a `CommandHandle` for further interaction with the newly created PTY. Connection and request timeouts can be specified.

```python
def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
           timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
           request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

--------------------------------

### Create PTY (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new PTY (pseudo-terminal) with specified configurations. It requires the PTY size and an output handler for incoming data. Optional arguments include user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. It returns an AsyncCommandHandle for interacting with the PTY.

```python
async def create(
        size: PtySize,
        on_data: OutputHandler[PtyOutput],
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle
# Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal).
# Arguments:
# - `size`: Size of the PTY
# - `on_data`: Callback to handle PTY data
# - `user`: User to use for the PTY
# - `cwd`: Working directory for the PTY
# - `envs`: Environment variables for the PTY
# - `timeout`: Timeout for the PTY in **seconds**
# - `request_timeout`: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
# Returns:
# Handle to interact with the PTY
```

--------------------------------

### Run Command in Background (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the sandbox and runs it in the background. It returns a `CommandHandle` that can be used to monitor the command's progress or retrieve its results later using `CommandHandle.wait`.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Pty Constructor and Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.3/commands/page.mdx

Initializes the Pty module for pseudo-terminal interactions and provides a method to create new PTY instances. The creation process requires specific options and returns a CommandHandle for managing the PTY. Dependencies include Transport, ConnectionConfig, and PtyCreateOpts.

```typescript
new Pty(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Pty
create(opts: PtyCreateOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to Running Command (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Connects to a command that is already running in the sandbox, identified by its process ID. It returns a CommandHandle for further interaction, such as waiting for completion using `CommandHandle.wait`.

```typescript
connect(pid: number, opts?: CommandConnectOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Command Request Timeout Configuration - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.1/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional configuration property for setting the request timeout in milliseconds for API requests. The default timeout is 30,000 milliseconds (30 seconds). This is useful for controlling the responsiveness of command-related operations.

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
// Default
30_000 // 30 seconds
```

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) within the sandbox. Configurable with size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. Returns a `CommandHandle` for interaction.

def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",

           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) in the sandbox. Configurable with PTY size, user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. Returns a CommandHandle for interacting with the new PTY.

```python
def create(size: PtySize,
           user: Username = "user",
           cwd: Optional[str] = None,
           envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,

--------------------------------

### Set Command Timeout

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies a timeout duration in milliseconds for a command's execution. If the command exceeds this time limit, it will be terminated. The default timeout is 60,000 milliseconds (60 seconds).

```typescript
optional timeoutMs: number;

// Default
// 60_000 // 60 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### Commands Constructor and Connection

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.3/commands/page.mdx

Initializes the Commands module and establishes a connection to a running command using its process ID. The CommandHandle returned can be used to wait for the command to complete and retrieve its results. Dependencies include Transport and ConnectionConfig.

```typescript
new Commands(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Commands
connect(pid: number, opts?: CommandConnectOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for starting a new command within the sandbox environment. This includes configuration for running commands in the background, setting the working directory, defining environment variables, and providing callbacks for standard output and error streams. It also includes options for request and command timeouts, and the user to run the command as.

```typescript
background?: boolean;
cwd?: string;
envs?: Record<string, string>;
onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
requestTimeoutMs?: number;
timeoutMs?: number;
user?: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Command Start Options in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/commands/page.mdx

Defines the options for starting a new command in the E2B environment. This includes settings for running commands in the background, specifying the working directory (cwd), environment variables (envs), and callbacks for standard error (onStderr) and standard output (onStdout). It also supports request and command timeouts, and specifying the user to run the command as.

```ts
{
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Pty Module Methods (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Contains methods for managing pseudo-terminals (PTYs) in the sandbox. `kill` terminates a PTY by its PID. `send_stdin` sends byte data to a PTY's input. `create` starts a new PTY with specified size, callbacks, user, and environment. `resize` adjusts the dimensions of an existing PTY. These methods may involve timeouts and depend on types like `PtySize`, `OutputHandler`, `PtyOutput`, `Username`, `Dict`, and `AsyncCommandHandle`.

```python
async def kill(self, pid: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> bool:
    """Kill PTY."""
    pass
```

```python
async def send_stdin(self, pid: int,
                     data: bytes,
                     request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Send input to a PTY."""
    pass
```

```python
async def create(
        self,
        size: PtySize,
        on_data: OutputHandler[PtyOutput],
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle:
    """Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal)."""
    pass
```

```python
async def resize(self, pid: int,
                 size: PtySize,
                 request_timeout: Optional[float] = None):
    """Resize PTY."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Request Timeout Configuration in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/commands/page.mdx

Defines an optional request timeout in milliseconds for API requests. This configuration is part of command request options and defaults to 30,000 milliseconds (30 seconds).

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
```

--------------------------------

### CommandRequestOpts Interface

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.7.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for making requests related to commands. This interface is extended by `CommandStartOpts` and primarily includes the `requestTimeoutMs` property for setting timeouts on API requests.

```typescript
interface CommandRequestOpts {
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Pseudo-Terminal (PTY) (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Asynchronously creates a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) within the sandbox. It requires the PTY size and an output handler for data. Optional parameters include the user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. It returns an AsyncCommandHandle to interact with the created PTY.

```python
async def create(
        size: PtySize,
        on_data: OutputHandler[PtyOutput],
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle

```

--------------------------------

### Python: Create New PTY

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.3/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) within the sandbox. Requires PTY size and an output handler. Optional arguments include user, working directory, environment variables, and timeouts. Returns an `AsyncCommandHandle`.

```python
async def create(
        size: PtySize,
        on_data: OutputHandler[PtyOutput],
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle:
    """
    Start a new PTY (pseudo-terminal).

    **Arguments**:

    - `size`: Size of the PTY
    - `on_data`: Callback to handle PTY data
    - `user`: User to use for the PTY
    - `cwd`: Working directory for the PTY
    - `envs`: Environment variables for the PTY
    - `timeout`: Timeout for the PTY in **seconds**
    - `request_timeout`: Timeout for the request in **seconds**

    **Returns**:

    Handle to interact with the PTY
    """
```

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.2/commands/page.mdx

The `requestTimeoutMs` property within interfaces like `CommandRequestOpts` and `CommandStartOpts` specifies the maximum duration, in milliseconds, for API requests. It has a default value of 60,000 milliseconds (60 seconds), providing a sensible timeout for network operations.

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

// Default
60_000 // 60 seconds
```

### Pty Create API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new Pseudo-Terminal (PTY) with specified configuration.

```APIDOC
## POST /pty/create

### Description
Starts a new Pseudo-Terminal (PTY) with specified configuration.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/pty/create

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the PTY in seconds (default: 60).
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Request Body
- **size** (PtySize) - Required - Size of the PTY (e.g., `{"rows": 24, "cols": 80}`).
- **on_data** (OutputHandler[PtyOutput]) - Required - Callback to handle PTY data.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to use for the PTY (default: "user").
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - Working directory for the PTY.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables for the PTY.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **command_handle** (AsyncCommandHandle) - Handle to interact with the PTY.

#### Response Example
{
  "command_handle": {
    "pid": 12345,
    "kill": "POST /pty/12345/kill",
    "send_stdin": "POST /pty/12345/stdin",
    "resize": "POST /pty/12345/resize"
  }
}
```

Kills a Pseudo-terminal (PTY) process identified by its process ID (pid). It can optionally accept connection options like `requestTimeoutMs`. Returns a promise that resolves to a boolean indicating if the PTY was successfully killed or not found.

```TypeScript
kill(
   pid: number,
   opts?: Pick<ConnectionOpts, "requestTimeoutMs">
): Promise<boolean>
```

### Run Command (Foreground)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new command and waits for it to finish executing, returning the result.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox/run

### Description
Starts a new command in the sandbox and waits for it to complete execution. Returns a `CommandResult` object containing the command's output and exit code.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/sandbox/run`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **cmd** (str) - Required - The command to execute.
- **background** (bool) - Optional - Set to `False` or omit for foreground execution.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables to set for the command.
- **user** (str) - Optional - The username to run the command as. Defaults to 'user'.
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - The working directory for the command.
- **on_stdout** (Callable[[str], None]) - Optional - A callback function to process standard output in real-time.
- **on_stderr** (Callable[[str], None]) - Optional - A callback function to process standard error in real-time.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - The maximum time in seconds to wait for the command to connect. `0` means no timeout. Defaults to 60 seconds.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - The maximum time in seconds for the request itself. 

### Request Example
```json
{
  "cmd": "echo hello world",
  "timeout": 30
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **stdout** (str) - The standard output of the command.
- **stderr** (str) - The standard error of the command.
- **exit_code** (int) - The exit code of the command.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "stdout": "hello world\n",
  "stderr": "",
  "exit_code": 0
}
```
```

kill(pid: number, opts?: Pick<ConnectionOpts, "requestTimeoutMs">): Promise<boolean>
